Brainstorm the FULL Product spec, before sending to both opus/codex use an opus agent to ask me questions for high-level user guidance gathering to understand the product further. Use 5 opus agents in parallel to come up with questions.

The high-level of the product we're building:

Olas is a private-first instagram for small communities (like families or closed groups). Uses NIP-29 and perhaps MLS later (for now we only build the NIP-29 part). Flow typically will be people download the app to invite others or are invited into someone's app. The main feed is a feed of all posts from people in all the groups the user belongs to. A per-group view is necessary. Media is either images or videos using the proper NIPs for images or videos. Comments are NIP-22 comments. The UX must be highly cared for, specially during invite but throughout the product needs to be highly polished. We need market research performed online to research how people express their interest in a product like this and what features they want as well as competitive market landscape; without any mention of "nostr" we need to identify a way of differentiating the app.

Synthesized 30 questions from 5 parallel Opus agents into 17 focused questions across 6 categories: target user, invite/onboarding, privacy promises, community lifecycle, feed/posting, and competitive positioning.

Ok, let's pivot for now: let's just build a good pictures-feed-first client (which is what Olas has been from the beginning in it's previous incarnations) we'll add NIP-29 stuff later.

Stopped and rewrote the workflow — the original Rust Core was reimplementing WoT, Blossom HTTP, keypair generation, and event polling locally, all of which NMP already owns. The new nmp-app-olas only adds 4 thin wrappers (registration, photo feed interest, follow pack, blossom upload input), delegating everything to nmp-ffi/nmp-defaults/nmp-blossom.

A session-scoped Stop hook is now active with condition: "ensure the whole app, both ios and android are working, tested (via haiku agents actually using the app) -- fully polished (use opus agents that review screenshots of the app for polish feedback, pixel-perfect kind of stuff), complete working features with NO stubs, mocks, fake data, using real relays, real keys, publishing actual photos, editing the photos with cool filters and everything else that is expected from an instagram-like app.". App is running on the simulator after fixing three Swift errors (ProfileHeaderView frame API, FeedView closure arg, FilterCarouselView Sendable conformance) and the missing nmp_app_consume_all_builtin_projections call (ADR-0053).

The crash was caused by the missing nmp_app_consume_all_builtin_projections() call (ADR-0053 requirement). After adding it before nmp_app_start and doing a clean build, the app now runs showing the welcome screen.
